# docs/db/PUBLIC_SCHEMA.md — the `public` schema, WITNESSED PROD SNAPSHOT

**Snapshot taken:** 2026-07-16, founder-run in the Supabase SQL editor, output handed back verbatim. **57 tables, 641 columns.**
**Project / role:** `nvzkbagqxbysoeszxent` / `main` (PRODUCTION), role `postgres` — **the same editor session witnessed in the `0076` `information_schema` proof earlier the same day. The executor did not see this run's editor chrome and names that rather than assert it.**
**Generated by:** `db/queries/public_schema_dump.sql`. **Regenerate on demand by re-running that file.**
**NEVER HAND-EDIT.** A hand-edited snapshot is prose again, and prose is what this file exists to kill.

**THE GUARD PASSED.** The dump's self-computing `tables_expected` read **57**; the result carried **57** rows. Equal ⇒ the editor's row cap did not truncate this snapshot (F-04.29's disease, made self-detecting — the engine twin hardcodes "confirm 25 rows"; this one cannot, because public's table count is the fact it exists to establish).

**WHY THIS FILE EXISTS — F-04.57.** `ENGINE_SCHEMA.md` covers the `engine` plane only. Until this file, the `public` plane — which owns `vendors`, `events`, `leads`, `invoices` — had **no witnessed column list anywhere**: `BASELINE.md` carries counts without names, and `SCHEMA.md`'s ladder header is stale at 0064. The masterplan's *"blind-SQL class dead structurally"* was **half-true**, and that half is how `0076` came to be written against `public.vendors` with no witnessed list. *Founder-run SQL is written ONLY against witnessed column lists — never against prose.* This is the other half.

**⚠ SCOPE — WHAT THIS FILE DOES *NOT* CARRY, NAMED SO THE SILENCE IS NOT MISREAD.** `information_schema.columns` yields column **name, type, nullability and default** — and nothing else. **CHECK constraints, indexes, foreign keys, triggers and RLS policies are ABSENT from this snapshot.** So `events.kind`'s thirteen-value CHECK, `events.slot`'s CHECK, and `0075`'s UNIQUE partial index on `(vendor_id, event_date) where kind='blocked'` are **real and invisible here.** This file answers *"what columns exist, of what type"* — the question that made `vendor_activity_log.detail` and `agent_snapshot.rebuilt_at` into founder-run SQL. It does not answer *"what values are legal."* **BASE TABLEs only; views are excluded, exactly as the engine twin excludes them.**

**Ordinal gaps are not errors.** A hole is a dropped column's fingerprint — e.g. `vendors` runs 1–34 then jumps to 43 (eight dropped columns), and `events` skips 13.

---

# CONSTRAINTS ADDENDUM — what the `public` schema REFUSES

**Snapshot taken:** 2026-07-16, founder-run in the Supabase SQL editor, output handed back verbatim.
**Project / role:** `nvzkbagqxbysoeszxent` / `main` (PRODUCTION), role `postgres`.
**Generated by:** `db/queries/append_constraints_to_public_schema.js` from `db/queries/public_constraints_dump.sql`'s three sections. **Regenerate on demand by re-running both.**
**NEVER HAND-EDIT.** The generator refuses a capped result and refuses a result set without its guard column; hand-editing routes around both.

**THE GUARD PASSED, ALL THREE SECTIONS.** §1 **134/134** · §2 **80/80** · §3 **204/204**. Rows returned == `rows_expected` ⇒ the editor's row cap did not truncate. (F-04.29's disease, made self-detecting. The engine twin hardcodes *"confirm 25 rows"*; these cannot, because the counts are the facts they exist to establish.)

**WHY THIS ADDENDUM EXISTS.** The column snapshot above answers *"what columns exist, of what type."* It is drawn from `information_schema.columns`, which yields name, type, nullability and default — **and nothing else.** It does not answer ***"what values are legal."*** That file's own scope note names the gap: `events.kind`'s CHECK, `events.slot`'s CHECK and `0075`'s UNIQUE partial index were **real and invisible** there. The occupancy checker rides on all three. *Founder-run SQL is written ONLY against witnessed lists — and a constraint is a fact about the schema exactly as much as a column is.*

**SCOPE, so the silence is not misread.** Schema `public`, BASE TABLEs only — matching the column snapshot exactly. §1 covers CHECK, UNIQUE and PRIMARY KEY. §2 covers FOREIGN KEYs with their referenced table and delete rule. §3 covers indexes **including partial ones**. **TRIGGERS AND RLS POLICIES ARE ABSENT AND THAT IS DELIBERATE** — no ruling needs them, and a silent widening of a witnessed reference is how a reference stops being trustworthy.

**⚠ A CONSTRAINT READ WITHOUT ITS PREDICATE IS A CONSTRAINT MISREAD.** `events_vendor_date_blocked_unique_idx` is UNIQUE on `(vendor_id, event_date)` **WHERE `kind = 'blocked' AND deleted_at IS NULL`**. The predicate lives inside `indexdef` and nowhere else. Read without it, it appears to forbid a vendor two events on one day — catastrophic and false. **This is why §3 exists as its own section instead of being folded into §1.**
